# Profanease — Full API Reference

> Modern, lightweight profanity detection & content moderation toolkit for JavaScript/TypeScript. Zero runtime dependencies for the core library (MCP server requires optional peer deps). Detects l33t speak ($h1t→shit), Unicode homoglyphs (Cyrillic а→a), zero-width characters, and repeated character obfuscation (fuuuck→fuck). Ships with 25 language packs (tree-shakeable). Content categories for fine-grained guard rails. Custom word list support with no defaults. Works everywhere: Node.js ≥20, browsers, Deno, Bun, Cloudflare Workers.

## Installation

```
npm install profanease
```

ESM: `import { Profanease } from 'profanease'`
CJS: `const { Profanease } = require('profanease')`

---

## Class: Profanease

### Constructor

```ts
new Profanease(options?: ProfaneaseOptions)
```

### Instance Methods

#### check(text: string): boolean
Returns `true` if the text contains any profane word. Fast boolean check — use this for guard rails.

```ts
filter.check('hello');       // false
filter.check('what the fuck'); // true
filter.check('$h1t');        // true (l33t speak detected at normalize='moderate')
```

#### clean(text: string): string
Returns the text with all profane words replaced according to the replacement strategy.

```ts
filter.clean('fuck you');     // '**** you'
filter.clean('$h1t');         // '****'
filter.clean('blow job');     // '**** ***' (phrase matching)
```

#### analyze(text: string): AnalysisResult
Returns detailed analysis with every match, categories, severity score, and cleaned text.

```ts
filter.analyze('fuck this shit');
// {
//   isProfane: true,
//   matches: [
//     { original: 'fuck', normalized: 'fuck', matched: 'fuck', index: 0, categories: ['profanity'] },
//     { original: 'shit', normalized: 'shit', matched: 'shit', index: 10, categories: ['profanity'] }
//   ],
//   categories: ['profanity'],
//   severity: 'mild',
//   cleaned: '**** this ****'
// }
```

#### addWords(words: string[], categories?: Category[]): void
Add words to the filter at runtime.

```ts
filter.addWords(['custom-term']);
filter.addWords(['slur-word'], [Category.SLUR]);
```

#### removeWords(words: string[]): void
Exclude words from filtering.

```ts
filter.removeWords(['hell', 'damn']);
```

#### wordsList(lang?: string): string[]
Get the active word list, or a specific language pack's words.

```ts
filter.wordsList();      // all loaded words
filter.wordsList('en');  // English pack words
```

### Static Methods

#### Profanease.custom(words: string[], options?): Profanease
Create a filter with ONLY your custom words — no built-in profanity lists loaded.

```ts
const filter = Profanease.custom(['spam', 'scam', 'phishing']);
filter.check('spam');    // true
filter.check('fuck');    // false (not in custom list)
```

Options accepted: `placeholder`, `replacement`, `normalize`, `categories`, `exclude`.

---

## Functional API

Stateless one-off functions — each creates a temporary Profanease instance:

```ts
import { check, clean, analyze } from 'profanease';
import en from 'profanease/langs/en';

check('text', { languages: [en] });    // boolean
clean('text', { languages: [en] });    // string
analyze('text', { languages: [en] });  // AnalysisResult
```

---

## Types

### ProfaneaseOptions

```ts
interface ProfaneaseOptions {
  languages?: LanguagePack[];       // Language packs to load (default: all)
  list?: string[];                  // Additional custom words
  exclude?: string[];               // Words to exclude from filtering
  emptyList?: boolean;              // Start with empty word list (default: false)
  placeholder?: string;             // Censor character (default: '*')
  replacement?: ReplacementStrategy; // How to replace matches (default: 'asterisk')
  normalize?: NormalizationLevel;   // Evasion detection level (default: 'moderate')
  categories?: Category[];          // Only filter these categories
}
```

### AnalysisResult

```ts
interface AnalysisResult {
  isProfane: boolean;       // Whether any profanity was found
  matches: MatchResult[];   // All matches with metadata
  categories: Category[];   // All unique categories found
  severity: Severity;       // 'none' | 'mild' | 'moderate' | 'severe'
  cleaned: string;          // Text with matches censored
}
```

### MatchResult

```ts
interface MatchResult {
  original: string;      // Word as it appeared in input
  normalized: string;    // Word after normalization
  matched: string;       // Word list entry it matched
  index: number;         // Start position in input text
  categories: Category[];// Categories of matched word
}
```

### Category (enum)

```ts
enum Category {
  PROFANITY = 'profanity',  // Swear words, expletives
  SEXUAL = 'sexual',        // Sexual terms, explicit content
  SLUR = 'slur',            // Racial, ethnic, identity slurs
  INSULT = 'insult',        // Personal insults, derogatory terms
  RELIGIOUS = 'religious',  // Religious profanity, blasphemy
  DRUGS = 'drugs',          // Drug references
  VIOLENCE = 'violence',    // Violent language
}
```

### NormalizationLevel

- `'none'` — Exact match only
- `'basic'` — Lowercase + strip accents (Fück → fuck)
- `'moderate'` — + l33t speak decoding ($h1t → shit, @$$ → ass)
- `'aggressive'` — + homoglyphs (Cyrillic→Latin) + zero-width char removal + repeated char collapse (fuuuck → fuck)

### ReplacementStrategy

- `'asterisk'` — Replace each character: fuck → ****
- `'grawlix'` — Comic-style: fuck → @#$%
- `'word'` — Label: fuck → [censored]
- `'full'` — Repeated placeholder: fuck → ****
- `(word: string) => string` — Custom function

### Severity Scoring

- `'none'` — No matches
- `'mild'` — 1-2 matches without severe categories
- `'moderate'` — 3+ matches, or sexual/violence content
- `'severe'` — Any slurs, or 5+ matches

---

## Language Packs

Import individually for tree-shaking:

```ts
import en from 'profanease/langs/en';  // English (2,983 words, categorized)
import es from 'profanease/langs/es';  // Spanish (190 words)
import fr from 'profanease/langs/fr';  // French (200 words)
import de from 'profanease/langs/de';  // German (179 words)
import all from 'profanease/langs/all'; // All 25 languages combined
```

Available: ar, cs, da, de, en, eo, es, fa, fi, fr, hi, hu, it, ja, ko, nl, no, pl, pt, ru, sv, th, tlh, tr, zh

Only the English pack (`en`) includes category tags. Other packs are flat string arrays.

---

## Core Utilities

Low-level functions exported for custom pipelines:

```ts
import { normalize, tokenize, replaceWord, decodeLeet, normalizeHomoglyphs } from 'profanease';

normalize('$h1t', 'moderate');        // 'shit'
normalize('café', 'basic');           // 'cafe'
decodeLeet('@$$h0l3');                // 'asshole'
normalizeHomoglyphs('\u0430ss');      // 'ass' (Cyrillic а → Latin a)
tokenize('hello world!');             // [{ value: 'hello', isWord: true, index: 0 }, ...]
replaceWord('fuck', 'grawlix');       // '@#$%'
```

---

## MCP Server (AI Integration)

Profanease ships an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server so AI assistants can use profanity filtering as a tool.

### Configuration

Add to your MCP client config (Claude Desktop, Cursor, etc.):

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "profanease": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["profanease-mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Available MCP Tools

#### profanease_check
Check if text contains profanity. Returns `{ isProfane: boolean }`.

Parameters:
- `text` (string, required) — Text to check
- `language` ("en" | "all", default: "en") — Language pack
- `normalize` ("none" | "basic" | "moderate" | "aggressive", default: "moderate")
- `categories` (string[], optional) — Only check these categories
- `custom_words` (string[], optional) — Additional words to filter
- `custom_only` (boolean, default: false) — Only use custom words, no built-in lists

#### profanease_clean
Clean text by replacing profane words. Returns `{ cleaned: string }`.

Parameters: Same as check, plus:
- `placeholder` (string, default: "*") — Censor character
- `replacement` ("asterisk" | "grawlix" | "word" | "full", default: "asterisk")

#### profanease_analyze
Full analysis with matches, categories, severity. Returns `AnalysisResult`.

Parameters: Same as check.

---

## Common AI Use Cases

### Guard Rails — Block harmful input before processing
```ts
const filter = new Profanease({ languages: [en], categories: [Category.SLUR, Category.VIOLENCE] });
if (filter.check(userInput)) {
  return 'Your message contains inappropriate content.';
}
```

### Content Moderation — Severity-based routing
```ts
const result = filter.analyze(userInput);
if (result.severity === 'severe') escalateToHuman(userInput);
else if (result.isProfane) autoClean(result.cleaned);
```

### Custom Brand Filter — No defaults
```ts
const brandFilter = Profanease.custom(['competitor-name', 'banned-phrase']);
```

### Multi-language Support
```ts
import en from 'profanease/langs/en';
import es from 'profanease/langs/es';
const filter = new Profanease({ languages: [en, es] });
```
